title: Share of people who have depressive disorders, by age
subtitle: Estimated share of people who have  [depressive disorders](#dod:depressive_disorders), whether or not they are diagnosed, based on representative surveys, medical data and statistical modelling.
caption:
Share of people who have depressive disorders, by age

Estimated share of people who have depressive disorders, whether or not they are diagnosed, based on representative surveys, medical data and statistical modelling.

The horizontal bar chart displays the estimated share of people with depressive disorders across various age groups for the world in 2023. The data reveals a distinct U-shaped distribution where prevalence is lowest among the youngest and oldest populations and highest among middle-aged and older adults. The lowest share is observed in the "Ages 5-14" group at 0.98%, followed by the "Age-standardized" metric at 3.81% and the "All ages" average at 4%. The "Ages 15-19" group shows a share of 4.55%, rising to 4.78% for "Ages 20-24" and 4.91% for "Ages 25-29". The highest prevalence rates are concentrated in the older age brackets. The "Ages 65-69" group records the peak value at 5.46%, closely followed by "Ages 60-64" at 5.4% and "Ages 70+" at 5.36%. The "Ages 55-59" group stands at 5.39%, while the "Ages 50-54" group is at 5.33%. Moving down the age scale, the "Ages 45-49" group is at 5.29%, "Ages 40-44" at 5.28%, "Ages 35-39" at 5.14%, and "Ages 30-34" at 5%. The values generally cluster between 5% and 5.5% for all groups aged 30 and above, excluding the standardized and total averages.
